title: confined_space_rescue
description: A worker has collapsed inside a permit-required confined space at an industrial facility.

timeline.title: Collapse Reported
timeline.description: Worker collapses inside a 10-metre-deep storage tank. Coworkers request entry to help. Establish incident command.

timeline.title: Atmospheric Testing
timeline.description: 4-gas monitor readings: oxygen 16%, LEL 8%, CO 200 ppm. Conditions immediately dangerous to life and health.

timeline.title: Rescue Team Staged
timeline.description: Technical rescue team on scene with supplied-air respirators, tripod, and retrieval system.

timeline.title: Entrant Secured
timeline.description: Rescue entrant has reached the patient. Patient is unresponsive; must be packaged for non-entry retrieval.

timeline.title: Patient Retrieved
timeline.description: Patient raised to surface. EMS performing post-rescue assessment. Debrief initiated.

node-1.title: Collapse Inside Confined Space
node-1.situation: A maintenance worker has collapsed inside a permit-required storage tank at a chemical plant. Two coworkers are preparing to enter without PPE to help. Atmospheric readings have not been taken.
node-1.question: What is your immediate priority as rescue team leader?

options.label: A
options.text: Stop all unauthorised entry attempts, establish incident command, and conduct atmospheric monitoring before any rescue entry.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow the coworkers to enter immediately — speed saves lives in a confined space.

options.label: C
options.text: Lower a rope to the victim and pull them out without entering.

node-2.title: Atmospheric Readings: IDLH Confirmed
node-2.situation: 4-gas monitor shows: O₂ 16% (low), LEL 8%, CO 200 ppm. Conditions are immediately dangerous to life and health (IDLH). The space requires supplied-air entry.
node-2.question: How do you proceed with the rescue?

options.label: A
options.text: Deploy a supplied-air rescue entrant with a full-body harness and retrieval line attached to a tripod system. Attendant remains outside.

options.label: B
options.text: Have the rescue entrant use an air-purifying respirator — it is faster to deploy.

options.label: C
options.text: Ventilate the space with a fan first, then send in an entrant with a SCBA and no retrieval line.

node-3.title: Non-Entry Retrieval Considered
node-3.situation: The entrant reaches the patient but finds them wedged against an internal baffle. A non-entry retrieval by rope may injure the patient further. The entrant's air supply is at 50%.
node-3.question: What is the correct course of action?

options.label: A
options.text: Carefully package the patient using a rescue basket and coordinate a controlled vertical lift with the tripod while monitoring entrant air supply.

options.label: B
options.text: Drag the patient by the harness immediately — air supply is the priority.

options.label: C
options.text: Send in a second entrant to help carry the patient without checking air supply levels.

node-4.title: Patient at Surface
node-4.situation: The patient has been extracted and is unresponsive with shallow respirations. EMS is on scene. The rescue entrant is showing signs of CO exposure after post-exit assessment.
node-4.question: How do you manage both casualties?

options.label: A
options.text: Provide high-flow oxygen to both casualties immediately and transport both to hospital for hyperbaric oxygen therapy evaluation.

options.label: B
options.text: Focus only on the original victim — the entrant feels well enough to continue working.

node-5.title: Scene Secured and Debriefed
node-5.situation: Both casualties are en route to hospital. The space has been locked out and the permit cancelled. An incident investigation is required.
node-5.question: What is the correct post-rescue action?

options.label: A
options.text: Preserve the scene, collect atmospheric monitoring records, complete the incident log, and initiate a formal investigation per confined space regulations.

options.label: B
options.text: Restore the space to service immediately once the victims are transported.

node-6.title: Scenario Complete
node-6.situation: Both patients received appropriate care. The confined space has been properly secured and the incident preserved for investigation. Your team followed permit-required confined space rescue protocols correctly, preventing additional casualties.
